statement: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget independently.
The system shall allow the IFA to control and monitor the balance.
The system shall allow specification of budget policies within the system.
The system shall alert users of certain violations.
The system shall allow the financial team to access the financial data of the teams.
The system shall allow administrators to view all transaction records.
The system shall allow referees to insert game events in real time using a mobile phone.
The system shall allow unregistered guests to view game result data.
The system shall allow users to export data for journalists or press.
The system shall allow team managers and team administrators to view data and perform offline analysis.
The system shall allow only teams to insert budget transactions and items.
The system shall restrict each team to access only its own financial data.
The system shall allow IFA to view all monetary information, receive alerts, and request details, and shall prevent IFA from modifying monetary data.
The system shall allow definition of scheduling policies for games.
The system shall allocate referees to games while respecting constraints such as not assigning the same referee to two games involving the same teams.
The system shall enable referees to input their availability and preferences, such as vacations, and consider them when scheduling.
The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule.
The system shall require a rationale explanation and approval by another IFA representative for any schedule override.
The system shall require that schedule changes be approved by two distinct users, the initiator and an approver.
The system shall enforce a minimum gap of days between consecutive matches for a team.
The system shall allow fans to subscribe to games or players and receive push notifications when events occur.
The system shall deliver notifications within the application or website and shall not send email notifications.
The system shall provide a single unified platform with a web interface for administrators and teams and mobile applications for referees and fans.
The system shall allow only official referees to update game data, ensuring it is the sole source of reliable game information.
The system shall send automatic reminders, such as five minutes before a game starts and for report submissions.
The system shall support up to 50,000 concurrent users, providing a response time of one to two seconds for most users and real‑time high‑priority performance for referees.
The system shall encrypt budget data to prevent leakage.
The system shall require each user to have a profile that includes a username.
The system shall support a team registration workflow in which a manager submits a request with documents, an IFA approves the request, and the team can then manage its resources.
The system shall retain all past scheduling archives.
The system shall allow definition of leagues and seasons, appointment and registration of referees, and setting of budget and scheduling policies.
The system shall allow the IFA to appoint and register referees.
